channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Artists can copyright works made with AI assistance: Agency
item.description: Artists can copyright work they create using tools powered by artificial intelligence, as long as sufficient human creativity is involved, according to a new report from the U.S. Copyright Office.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump settles suit with Meta over suspended accounts for $25M
item.description: Meta will pay President Trump $25 million to settle a 2021 lawsuit brought by the president over the social media company’s decision to suspend his accounts in the wake of the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol insurrection. The settlement was filed in federal court in California on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The rise of ‘influencer voice’: Why this TikTok creator accent is taking over the internet and maybe the world
item.description: The “influencer accent” is taking over TikTok. If you don’t know what I’m talking about, scroll through your FYP page and listen.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: AI assistants for lawyers are a booming business—with big risks
item.description: Illinois lawyer Mathew Kerbis markets himself as the Subscription Attorney, charging businesses and individual clients a monthly rate for legal advice and offering additional services like contract review and legal document drafting for a flat fee.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: 200 UK companies adopt a permanent four-day work week
item.description: Two years after participating in a groundbreaking four-day work week experiment, 200 companies in the UK have chosen to adopt the policy permanently, marking a significant shift in workplace norms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Windows 11 users with iPhones: if you’re jealous of Android’s Start menu integration, here’s some good news – Apple phones are now getting it
item.description: Microsoft is only testing the Start menu side panel with iPhones, but it likely won’t be long before it’s introduced to the release version of Windows 11.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: La IA está lista para renovar la experiencia del cliente
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) está preparada para hacerse cargo del recorrido del cliente para una amplia gama de productos en los próximos cinco años. Los CIO que ignoren la tendencia corren el riesgo de perder terreno frente a la competencia, advierten los expertos en IA y marketing.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Predicciones de TI: cinco cosas que no cambiarán en 2025
item.description: Cada mes de enero, los visionarios opinan sobre lo que será diferente. Igual de importantes, pero normalmente infravaloradas, son las tendencias que se perpetuarán hasta bien entrado el año que viene. He aquí un vistazo a lo que creo que seguirá igual para los líderes de TI en 2025.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bookshop.org is launching e-books to help local bookstores compete with Amazon’s Kindle
item.description: Andy Hunter decided something needed to be done about the endless rise of Amazon in 2018—the year that the e-commerce giant surpassed 50% of book sales in the U.S. market. “I was concerned at that rate of growth,” says founder and CEO of Bookshop.org.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Financial management QSMO growing in use, providers
item.description: Steve Nulter, a senior business analyst in the Quality Service Management Office, said more agencies are using financial management shared services. The post Financial management QSMO growing in use, providers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Chinese tech startup DeepSeek ’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT. DeepSeek’s AI assistant was the No.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to share a YouTube video starting at a specific time
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says these steps ensure that your friends and family get to the exact moment you want them to see. Try it out and streamline your sharing experience.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top 8 outages of 2024
item.description: Network outages continued to plague organizations of all sizes in 2024, with major incidents exposing vulnerabilities in digital infrastructure and the growing complexity of IT environments, according to ThousandEyes’ recent Internet outage report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cell Service Worsening Across US: New Study Reveals Alarming Trends
item.description: A new J.D. Power study reveals a significant increase in reported wireless network problems across the US, with 22% more issues per 100 phone uses in just one year.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: What enterprises need to know about DeepSeek’s game-changing R1 AI model
item.description: Two years ago, OpenAI’s ChatGPT launched a new wave of AI disruption that left the tech industry reassessing its future. Now within the space of a week a small Chinese startup called DeepSeek appears to have pulled off a similar coup, this time at OpenAI’s expense.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Businesses get their own version of the Chrome Web Store
item.description: Though there are a variety of cool extensions for the Chrome browser, there are also malicious extensions that pose a security threat. To increase security, Google has now launched the Chrome Web Store for Enterprises, a new store specifically designed for business users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: iPhone users turn on to DeepSeek AI
item.description: As if from nowhere, OpenAI competitor DeepSeek has somersaulted to the top of the iPhone App Store chart, overtaking ChatGPT’s OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: RIGA, Latvia (AP) — Swedish prosecutors announced Sunday night that they have opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SD-WAN to gain AI-driven deployment, management capabilities
item.description: AI is set to make its mark on SD-WAN technology. For starters, generative AI capabilities will improve how enterprise IT teams deploy and manage their SD-WAN architecture. In addition, AI workloads have distinctive requirements that will influence SD-WAN connectivity choices.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Biggest Wi-Fi mistakes you can make on a plane
item.description: It's hard to imagine life without in-flight Wi-Fi these days – but sky-high web surfing comes with its own set of privacy concerns. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Crooks can't steal this motorcycle that hides in plain sight
item.description: A nondescript metal box transforms into a fully functional electric motorcycle that blurs the line between vehicle and urban infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Allstate sued for allegedly tracking and selling 45M Americans' location data
item.description: Many apps unrelated to location still ask users for tracking permission, and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit seeking to end this alleged practice by Allstate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts — though it is not clear if there are many buyers at those prices. TikTok was briefly unavailable to U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Data center growth puts unprecedented pressure on power grids
item.description: US data centers will need 55 gigawatts of new power capacity within the next five years, according to a report released this week by Bloom Energy. The report, based on a survey of 100 data center leaders, also shows that 30% of all sites will be using onsite power by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Robotic dog helps those facing mental health and cognitive challenges
item.description: A U.S. robotics company has developed "Jennie," a robotic pet powered by artificial intelligence that's designed to comfort those facing mental health challenges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The sickening truth: Healthcare data breaches reach all-time high
item.description: Healthcare records for more than 184 million Americans were breached in 2024, but you can take steps to minimize that risk. Kurt the CyberGuy explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
